recent_tools.name: Write
recent_tools.output:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Replace broken _ACTION_TARGET_PATTERNS lines (279-291) with valid ones.

The problem: raw strings r"..." can't contain unescaped " characters.
The fix: use \x22 for double-quote, \x27 for single-quote, \x60 for backtick.
These hex escapes survive inside r"..." strings and any linter.
"""
from pathlib import Path

f = Path(r"P:/.claude/hooks/unified_claim_verifier.py")
lines = f.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()

# Lines 279-291 (0-indexed: 278-290) are the broken block.
# Verify by checking the first and last line content.
assert "_ACTION_TARGET_PATTERNS" in lines[278], f"Line 279 unexpected: {lines[278]}"
assert lines[290].strip() == "]", f"Line 291 unexpected: {lines[290]}"

# Build replacement lines using hex escapes for quote chars.
# \x22 = "  \x27 = '  \x60 = `
# These are valid inside r"..." and no linter will touch them.
new_lines = []
new_lines.append("_ACTION_TARGET_PATTERNS = [")
new_lines.append('    # grepped/searched for X and/but/in ...')
new_lines.append(
    "    re.compile("
    "r" + repr("(?:grepped|searched|looked|grep(?:ped)?)\\s+for\\s+[\\x22\\x27\\x60]?(.+?)[\\x22\\x27\\x60]?\\s+(?:and|but|in)\\b")
    + ", re.IGNORECASE),"
)
new_lines.append('    # grepped/searched for X (bare word)')
new_lines.append(
    "    re.compile("
    "r" + repr("(?:grepped|searched|looked|grep(?:ped)?)\\s+for\\s+(\\S+)")
    + ", re.IGNORECASE),"
)
new_lines.append('    # fetched https://...')
new_lines.append(
    "    re.compile("
    "r" + repr("(?:fetched|hit|called|pinged)\\s+(https?://\\S+)")
    + ", re.IGNORECASE),"
)
new_lines.append('    # fetched the endpoint X')
new_lines.append(
    "    re.compile("
    "r" + repr("(?:fetched|hit|called|pinged)\\s+(?:the\\s+)?(?:endpoint|url|api)\\s+(\\S+)")
    + ", re.IGNORECASE),"
)
new_lines.append('    # grep returned nothing for X')
new_lines.append(
    "    re.compile("
    "r" + repr("grep\\s+(?:returned|found|showed)\\s+\\w+\\s+for\\s+(\\S+)")
    + ", re.IGNORECASE),"
)
new
